text: In this video, robot-assisted laparoscopic adrenalectomy will be demonstrated for a four centimeter pheochromocytoma. The objectives discussed in this video will be the case of a forty-four year old male with intermittent hypertension refractory to multiple antihypertensive agents, port placement, and some technical aspects of left robotic adrenalectomy. This patient underwent extensive endocrinologic workup and CT imaging revealed a four centimeter adrenal mass. Pheochromocytoma was at the top of the differential diagnosis.

The patient is positioned in the right lateral decubitus position for a robotic left adrenalectomy. Trocar placement is completed with the camera trocar in the left midclavicular line, and additional trocars for the robotic arms positioned superior, inferior, and lateral to the camera trocar. The procedure begins with medial reflection of the descending colon, followed by cephalad incision of the peritoneum lateral to the spleen, allowing superior medial reflection of the spleen. The fourth robotic arm applies gentle superior medial pressure on the spleen to develop the plane between the kidney and spleen.

The robotic fourth arm applies anterior pressure on the lower pole of the left kidney, facilitating the identification of the left gonadal vein. The surgeon dissects cephalad towards the left renal vein, dividing tissue medial to it for better exposure. The left adrenal vein is identified on the cephalad aspect of the left renal vein, ligated with two hemlock clips, and then divided.

Following the division of the adrenal vein, the surgeon divides the tissue inferior and medial to the adrenal gland. The robotic fourth arm is utilized for retraction of the adrenal gland to aid in its final removal. The adrenal specimen is placed in a retrieval bag and extracted from the surgical site.
